results.query: the design reviewer just flagged that two of my card slices overlap and the decomposition is wrong — do i hand-edit the arch doc or rerun something?
a card's plan has failed review three times in a row in my agentboard architecture run and it just won't pass, what now

how does a correction get routed back to the right stage in the architecture pipeline when a review finds something wrong
an upstream failure traced back to an architecture decision — where does the fix go and how do i pass it into the stage that has to redo the work?
the classification audit facts the architecture rests on turned out to be wrong. do i rerun research or just patch the architecture document?
i don't want corrections in /architecture to just stop and dump everything on me when something fails repeatedly. how should that be handled
should the architecture flow ever edit the spec directly when a review finding points at a missing requirement, or does that go somewhere else?
the reviewer keeps bouncing this card back. at what point does it stop retrying and actually start investigating the real root cause?
need to rework a design decision in the arch doc after the review pass — whats the right way to feed that correction back in instead of just editing the file by hand
run the architecture pipeline on docs/specs/2026-05-23-checkout.md and create the cards

how many cards are left on the cleanup board and how many are finished?
review this pull request for correctness bugs and security issues
there's a typo in the architecture document's overview heading, just fix the spelling
explain how the classification audit decides whether work is L1, L2, or L3
orchestrate the board — kick off planning and review on the cards that are ready
